title: APS Accountability Record
description: A signed record attesting a single boundary decision (allow/deny/halt) over one agent action and whether that action executed. The shape is derived from the agent-passport-system receipt primitives, not a parallel invention: action_ref is the content-addressed request identity (computeActionRef, draft-pidlisnyi-aps-00 §4.1), action_digest is the detached-payload commitment (the digestOf pattern used by decisionReceipt), agent_did/action_ref follow the execution-envelope field convention, and sig is Ed25519 over the RFC 8785 (JCS) canonical bytes of the record with the `sig` field omitted. WHAT THIS RECORD DOES NOT PROVE: it does not attest outcome quality, correctness, success, or the real-world effect of the action; it does not prove that any settlement occurred (settlement_ref and settlement_rail are opaque, rail-neutral correlation hints, never settlement proof); and it asserts nothing beyond the recorded boundary decision and the executed flag. The record is silent on everything else, and silence here is not a denial. The `decision` here is the boundary verdict on a concrete action (allow|deny|halt), deliberately distinct from decisionReceipt's policy-evaluation vocabulary (permit|deny|narrow).

spec_version.description: Version of the accountability-record schema this record conforms to, e.g. '0.1.0'.

action_ref.description: Content-addressed request identity: lowercase hex SHA-256 of the JCS canonicalization of the action tuple {agentId: agent_did, actionType: action.type, scopeRequired: action.scope, timestamp: normalizeTimestamp(action.timestamp)}. Recomputable from the inline `action` (when present) exactly as agent-passport-system computeActionRef does. Correlation key: because the timestamp is second-precision, distinct actions can share an action_ref; it is NOT a unique event id, and uniqueness and anti-replay are verifier-side concerns. Not a proof of what the action did.

sha256.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the JCS canonicalization of the `action` object.

action_digest.description: Detached-payload commitment to the recorded action. The `action` object MAY be omitted from the record; a holder of the payload recomputes this digest over the `action` object to bind it.

timestamp.description: When the action was requested (ISO 8601). Normalized to second precision when folded into action_ref.

action.description: OPTIONAL inline action payload (detached-payload pattern). The field is named `action`, matching the inline `action` object on the SDK ActionReceipt/CommerceActionReceipt; this is a different, smaller shape (type/scope/timestamp) than ActionReceipt.action, and it deliberately reuses a name that also collides with the ActivityStreams `action` verb-object, which this schema does not use. When present, sha256(JCS(action)) MUST equal action_digest.sha256 and action_ref MUST be recomputable from it. When absent, action_digest and action_ref stand as commitments to a payload held out-of-band.

signer_did.description: DID URI of the RECORDER that signed this record. Distinct from agent_did.

agent_did.description: DID URI of the agent whose action is recorded. Distinct from signer_did (the recorder).

delegation_ref.description: Opaque reference to the delegation, or delegation-chain root, that authorized the action. Recorder-asserted pointer; presence proves neither validity/expiry of the delegation nor principal consent/benefit.

principal_ref.description: Beneficiary attribution: reference (DID or opaque id) to the principal on whose behalf the action was taken and to whom accountability runs. Recorder-asserted pointer; presence proves neither validity/expiry of the delegation nor principal consent/benefit.

decision.description: Boundary decision recorded. 'allow' permitted the action, 'deny' refused it, 'halt' stopped it. This is the enforcement-boundary verdict, nothing more.

executed.description: Boundary-observed execution status as asserted by the recorder, not outcome truth. Recorded separately from `decision` and not derived from it; a denied or halted action is normally executed=false, but each field is asserted independently.

issued_at.description: When the recorder issued this record (ISO 8601). Part of the signed bytes.

settlement_ref.description: OPTIONAL opaque settlement correlation string. Rail-neutral: an identifier only, never proof that settlement occurred.

settlement_rail.description: OPTIONAL free-text label naming a settlement rail. Rail-neutral by construction: an uninterpreted label with no rail-specific semantics attached by this schema.

sig.description: Ed25519 signature (lowercase hex, 64 bytes) over the UTF-8 bytes of the JCS canonicalization of this record with the `sig` field omitted.

sig_alg.description: Signature algorithm: Ed25519 over RFC 8785 (JCS) canonical bytes.
